subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, EC, Eurostat, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI, MONEYVAL) + Latvian official (CSB, Bank of Latvia, MFA/PMLP) + Latvian independent press (LSM).
A solid-institution EU/eurozone NATO frontline state; the dominant tails are a Russia-border war/hybrid risk and a severe demographic decline.

Standout features: low debt, EU-standard institutions, top-tier alliance value, against a Russia-border war tail, a Russian-minority cleavage, and steep depopulation. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone on security framing.

economic.notes: Low debt and a cleaned-up banking sector, against a defense-driven rising deficit and lost Russia transit.

fiscal_state.notes: Low debt (~47% GDP) but a deficit rising toward ~3.3% on 5%-of-GDP defence; the IMF flags eroding space. (EC forecast; IMF Art IV 2025)

inflation.notes: Eased to ~2.3% with a small 2026 energy-driven uptick. (Eurostat; Bank of Latvia)

banking_stability.notes: Sound, with the prior money-laundering legacy largely resolved and a strong recent MONEYVAL evaluation. (MONEYVAL; LSM)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Timber/transit/services plus manufacturing, EU-integrated; the lost Russia transit/trade re-orienting to the EU/Nordics. (CSB; LSM)

institutional.notes: Strong, EU-standard institutions, with the AML legacy a watch.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong and EU-standard, with EC-flagged procurement-corruption risk and an AML watch. (EC Rule of Law 2025; WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: CSB is credible and Eurostat-aligned. (CSB; Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Capable and EU-fund-absorbing, with small-state capacity limits. (EC)

press_freedom.notes: Free (RSF ~15th), with a Russian-speaker info-access caveat. (RSF 2025; LSM)

political_social.notes: A stable democracy with strong liberties, against a Russian-minority cleavage and steep depopulation.

political_stability.notes: A stable EU democracy, orderly, with a strong anti-Russia consensus. (EC; V-Dem)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong and EU-standard. (EC Rule of Law; V-Dem)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: EU norms, but with a large legacy 'non-citizen' population and tightened 2022-25 residency/language rules pushing out Russian nationals. (PMLP; RFE/RL)

social_cohesion.notes: A real Russian-minority cleavage, with integration/language gaps and info-space vulnerability. (EC; State Security Service)

demographics.notes: Among the EU's steepest declines - heavy emigration, low fertility (~1.36), and deep ageing. (CSB; Eurostat)

geopolitical.notes: Top-tier alliance value, squarely offset by a Russia-border war/hybrid tail.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO/EU frontline stalwart and top-tier defence spender (toward 5% of GDP), hardline on Russia. (NATO; LSM)

conflict_involvement.notes: Frontline to the Russia-Ukraine war with a ~400km Russia/Belarus border, Baltic drone incidents, and undersea-cable sabotage - a real elevated tail. (Euronews; Atlantic Council)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account. (EC)

physical_practical.notes: Decent infrastructure and energy security, against an underfunded health system.

climate.notes: A Baltic high-latitude winner near/mid-term (longer growing season, milder winters, strong readiness), with the gain easing long-term (AMOC/coastal tail). (ND-GAIN; Burke et al.)

healthcare.notes: Universal but underfunded (the Baltics' weaker), with unmet need and medic shortages. (OECD Health at a Glance 2025)

infrastructure.notes: Decent and EU-funded, with the Feb-2025 Baltic grid desync from Russia an energy-security gain (a sabotage-target risk). (Atlantic Council; ECFR)

crime_safety.notes: Low-to-moderate; the hybrid/border dimension is scored separately. (general)

summary: A low-debt EU and eurozone democracy with solid, EU-standard institutions, a cleaned-up banking sector, a free press, and top-tier NATO alliance value as a frontline state. The countervailing tails are a Russia-border war and hybrid risk (a long frontier, Baltic drone incidents, undersea-cable sabotage), a real Russian-minority cleavage compounded by tightened residency rules, an underfunded health system, and among the EU's steepest demographic declines. Net, a balanced outcome distribution.
